input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
The most likely cause is that you never set the page's **`BindingContext`** to your ViewModel. Without it, `{Binding …}` has nothing to resolve against.

**Fix — set it in code-behind:**

```csharp
public partial class MyPage : ContentPage
{
    public MyPage()
    {
        InitializeComponent();
        BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
    }
}
```

**Or in XAML:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

**Other things to check:**
1. Your ViewModel properties must raise `PropertyChanged` (implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` or inherit `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit).
2. Property names in `{Binding …}` must exactly match the C# property names (case-sensitive).
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank even though the ViewModel has values. Looking at their XAML, the bindings look correct syntactically, but the most likely issue is that they haven't set the `BindingContext`.

There are also a couple of other common causes:
1. `BindingContext` not set
2. ViewModel properties not implementing `INotifyPropertyChanged`

The most likely culprit given the XAML shown is #1 — there's no `BindingContext` assigned anywhere.
